---
title: "Tapestry × ASTRA"
occasion: Lightcone UX Hack
description: Visualizing research decisions with progressive disclosure
draft: true
author:
  name: Cail Daley
date: March 31, 2026
date-format: long

output-file: 2603_Lightcone_UX.html

format:
  revealjs:
    width: 1920
    height: 1080
    theme: [default, custom.scss]
    slide-number: true
    fig-align: center
    include-after-body: assets/no_footer_on_titleslide.html
    footer: Cail Daley | Tapestry × ASTRA | Lightcone UX Hack | March 31, 2026
    history: false
    hash: true
    pdf-separate-fragments: true
    pdf-max-pages-per-slide: 1
    template-partials:
      - title-slide.html
    center-title-slide: false

html-math-method:
  method: mathjax
  url: https://cdn.jsdelivr.net/npm/mathjax@3/es5/tex-svg-full.js
include-in-header: assets/mathjax.html
---


## Getting out ahead of your skis

::: {style="font-size:0.85em"}

> I find if I am making progress on more than two or three architectural concepts at a time I can't hold the proper context in my own head, and **getting out ahead of your skis is the most dangerous thing in agent coding.** It is dangerous because it is tempting, and seemingly works at first, but very quickly breaks down in a way that is hard to recover from.
>
> If you care at all about what you're building, if you care to apply your own will to the result, if you are crafting a vision from your own mind and not the mind of the shoggoth, take care to not let your agents run ahead of you.

::: {style="text-align:right;font-size:0.75em;opacity:0.7"}
— @irl_danB
:::

:::

::: {style="font-size:0.7em;opacity:0.7;margin-top:-0.3em"}
[x.com/irl_danB/status/2038656769556373562](https://x.com/irl_danB/status/2038656769556373562)
:::

Back from Moriond...


## A running analysis with ASTRA decisions and felt evidence

- **Kinematic lensing** — extracting gravitational shear from galaxy velocity fields

- ASTRA spec with **7 decisions** across 1 sub-analysis (`build_mocks`)

- **Snakemake backend** generates scatter-gather rules from the spec
  - 10-seed mock sweep runs end-to-end on SLURM

- **Felt fibers** carry the evidence and reasoning — 19 closed tapestry nodes

- The question: **how do decisions and evidence present together?**


## The tapestry is a knowledge graph of research fibers

- Each fiber is a concern: task, decision, question, finding — connected in a **DAG**

- Evidence nodes carry **plots, metrics, judgment**; fog mechanic reveals on click

- Already works — but **doesn't know about ASTRA decisions**

```{=html}
<pre style="font-size:0.55em;line-height:1.7;background:#f8f8f5;padding:1em 1.5em;border-left:4px solid #475569;border-radius:8px;box-shadow:none"><code>✓ Intensity profile          ✓ Galaxy generation          ? Sigma floor suspicious
│                             │                            │
├─ ✓ Noise model verified    ├─ ✓ MIRoRS residuals        └─ ○ Multiplicative bias
│  └─ ✓ Likelihood peaks     └─ ✓ Shear reference frame
│     at truth                   └─ ✓ Phi symmetry
└─ ✓ Covariance matches
   └─ ✓ Covariance model</code></pre>
```


## ASTRA decisions are formal; fibers are the reasoning behind them

:::::::::::::: {.columns}
::: {.column style="width:48%"}

### ASTRA says *what*

```{.yaml style="font-size:0.6em"}
noise_scaling:
  default: intensity_weighted
  options:
    intensity_weighted:
      label: "σ = δv √(I_peak/I)"
    constant:
      excluded: true
      excluded_reason: "Not physical"
```

:::
::: {.column style="width:48%"}

### Fibers say *why*

```{.text style="font-size:0.6em"}
● noise-model-correctly-2205184f
  "Linear scaling exact (ratio=2.0),
   spatial variation 5.3× from
   inclination"
  Status: closed
  Evidence: 3 plots, 4 metrics
```

:::
::::::::::::::

- Decisions define **what was chosen**; fibers carry **the investigation and judgment**

- **Complementary, not competing** — tags wire them together


## Don't restructure the data — restructure the view

::: {.spaced-bullets}

- Fibers stay organic. **Tags wire them to decisions.**

  `evidence:noise_scaling` on a fiber → it shows up under that decision

- The tapestry dashboard **assembles views** from three sources:

:::

<br>

:::::::::::::: {.columns}
::: {.column style="width:33%;text-align:center"}

### ASTRA spec
decisions, alternatives,<br> insights

*the structure*

:::
::: {.column style="width:34%;text-align:center"}

### Felt fibers
evidence, investigation,<br> judgment

*the reasoning*

:::
::: {.column style="width:33%;text-align:center"}

### Execution state
outputs exist,<br> stale, running

*the pipeline*

:::
::::::::::::::


## The spine follows the structure of a scientific paper

<br>

```{=html}
<pre style="font-size:0.65em;line-height:1.7;background:#f8f8f5;padding:1em 1.5em;border-left:4px solid #475569;border-radius:8px;box-shadow:none"><code>Introduction    →    Data & Methods    →    Results         →    Conclusions
                          │                    │                      │
                     build_mocks          likelihood             calibration
                     methodology          inference              synthesis</code></pre>
```

<br>

::: {.spaced-bullets}

- **Horizontal axis** = scientific questions, aligned with sub-analyses

- **Vertical axis** = investigation depth

- More investigation makes it **taller**, not wider

- The spine is **fixed, immovable** — branches expand below

:::


## Progressive disclosure: three levels, two clicks

<br>

| Level | Shows | Example |
|:------|:------|:--------|
| **L0 — Spine** | Sub-analysis verdict + status | `Mocks` [✓]{.status-resolved} `5/7 decisions resolved` |
| **L1 — Decision** | Verdict sentence + evidence count | `noise_scaling: intensity_weighted` [✓]{.status-resolved} `— "Linear scaling exact"` |
| **L2 — Evidence** | Full fiber: plots, metrics, upstream | The actual investigation |

<br>

::: {.spaced-bullets}

- Fiber **outcomes ARE the declarative titles** — no extra writing

- Collapsed branches show count + worst status, **never blank**

- **URL-encoded state** — bookmarkable views

:::


## The default view is the delta

::: {.spaced-bullets}

- **Returning researcher**: what changed since I last looked?

- Nodes with new evidence **start revealed**; everything else **starts fogged**

- The existing fog mechanic already supports this — the change is making initial visibility **data-driven** (by recency) instead of interaction-driven (by click)

- Items stay revealed until **reviewed and validated by a human**, then fold back

- This is the **BLUF** (bottom line up front) pattern:

:::

```{=html}
<pre style="font-size:0.6em;line-height:1.8;background:#f8f8f5;padding:1em 1.5em;border-left:4px solid #475569;border-radius:8px;box-shadow:none"><code>Since last session:
  ✓  10 mocks generated (build_mocks complete)
  ?  sigma_floor still unresolved — blocking calibration
  ○  Next decision needed: mask_strategy</code></pre>
```


## Four epistemic states, shape over color

<br>

| State | Icon | Meaning |
|:------|:----:|:--------|
| Resolved | [✓]{.status-resolved} | Decision closed with evidence |
| Open | [○]{.status-open} | No evidence yet / in progress |
| Suspicious | [?]{.status-suspicious} | Evidence exists but judgment uncertain |
| Blocked | [✕]{.status-blocked} | Depends on unresolved upstream |

<br>

::: {.spaced-bullets}

- **Shape > color** — three channels: icon + muted color + text label

- **Pipeline health ≠ scientific judgment** — a mock can be generated (pipeline ✓) but suspicious (science ?)

- Reserve saturated red for **genuine failures only**

:::


## Overview first, zoom and filter, details on demand

::: {.spaced-bullets}

- **Shneiderman's visual information seeking mantra** (1996)

  The three-level disclosure maps directly onto it

- **Spine** = overview — what's decided, what's open

- **Decisions** = zoom and filter — drill into what needs attention

- **Evidence** = details on demand — plots, metrics, investigation chain

- **Cross-cutting decisions just work**: `shear_frame` has evidence from 3 different sub-analyses — all tagged, all surface when drilling in

:::

::: {style="font-size:0.7em;opacity:0.7;margin-top:1em"}
B. Shneiderman, "The Eyes Have It: A Task by Data Type Taxonomy for Information Visualizations," *IEEE Symposium on Visual Languages*, 1996
:::


## Conclusions first, evidence underneath

::: {.spaced-bullets}

- **Barbara Minto's Pyramid Principle**: lead with the answer, group supporting arguments, let evidence follow

- Applied to every level of the tapestry:

  - Spine headline: **verdict**, not description
  - Decision title: **"Linear scaling exact (ratio=2.0)"**, not "Noise model"
  - Evidence: the plots, only when you drill in

- The headline sequence **reads as a coherent argument**

- This is what makes it a **briefing**, not a dashboard

:::


## The tapestry is a briefing, not a dashboard

::: {.spaced-bullets}

- **Lead with science**, not pipeline — the overview shows decisions, not jobs

- **Default to delta** — what changed since last session

- **Group by question**, not pipeline step — "Are mocks physical?" not "Stage 1"

- **The returning researcher** needs: what's decided, what changed, what needs my attention — and *judgment*, not just data

- An agent could generate the entry briefing, walk the spine, flag what's new

:::


## Implementation: tags → spine → delta → drill-in

| Phase | What | Effort |
|:------|:-----|:-------|
| **1. Tags** | `evidence:decision_id` on existing fibers | Data prep, no viewer changes |
| **2. Spine** | Horizontal layout for tier-1 nodes in paper order | New layout mode in portolan |
| **3. Delta** | Initial visibility driven by mtime recency | Small change to fog logic |
| **4. Drill-in** | Click spine → show ASTRA decisions + evidence | Needs ASTRA metadata in export |

::: {.spaced-bullets}

- Build on **Tessera components**: InsightCard, EvidenceBlock, VerificationBadge

- **ASTRA insights** bridge the formal claim and the full investigation

:::
